findings.title: URMA failover hooks leak into the common worker API
findings.impact: Adding RecordUrmaDataPlaneResult, FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt, and SetUrmaDataPlaneFailureCallback as no-op virtuals on IClientWorkerCommonApi makes every worker implementation carry URMA-specific failover concepts even when it cannot use URMA. Future transport-specific recovery logic will have to understand these hidden optional hooks or add more no-op methods, making the common API harder to reason about.
findings.fix: Keep the common worker API transport-neutral: move these hooks behind a small URMA-capable interface/observer owned by the remote worker API, or inject a nullable failover recorder into the UB send/get paths instead of widening IClientWorkerCommonApi.

findings.title: Test-only tracker accessor is part of the production API
findings.impact: GetCurrentSampleCountForTest exposes internal counter state on the production tracker solely for tests. Because this class is built as a normal client library, future code can accidentally depend on a test-oriented, implementation-specific snapshot and make later changes to the tracker state model harder.
findings.fix: Hide the accessor behind a test-only build guard or friend the unit test fixture, and keep the production surface limited to RecordUrmaResult, FinishSwitchAttempt, ResetWindow, and operational state needed by runtime logging.
